text: Security is a critical part of web development that protects both users and data. Using HTTPS ensures that information sent between a browser and server is encrypted and safe from interception. Authentication systems, such as passwords or two-factor verification, verify user identities and prevent unauthorised access. Input validation is equally important; it checks that all user-submitted data is safe and properly formatted, stopping attackers from inserting malicious code. Common vulnerabilities, like SQL injection or cross-site scripting, can occur when these precautions are ignored. By implementing strong security measures from the start, developers can build trust and reduce the risk of attacks.
